Package io.mdudel.zenoh.purejava.wire.messages

Class Hello

java.lang.Object
io.mdudel.zenoh.purejava.wire.messages.Hello
public final class Hello extends Object
The Zenoh scouting-level HELLO message.

A HELLO is either the unicast reply to a Scout, or a periodic multicast advertisement of a node's presence. It carries the sender's role, ZenohID, and the network locators it accepts sessions on.

Wire layout (from the reference source ASCII diagram in commons/zenoh-protocol/src/scouting/hello.rs):

  7 6 5 4 3 2 1 0
 +-+-+-+-+-+-+-+-+
 |Z|X|L|  HELLO  |    header byte: id=0x02 | L(bit5) | Z(bit7)
 +-+-+-+---------+
 |    version    |
 +---------------+
 |zid_len|X|X|wai|    (*)
 +-+-+-+-+-+-+-+-+
 ~     [u8]      ~    ZenohID bytes (length = 1 + zid_len; ALWAYS present)
 +---------------+
 ~   [Locator]   ~    if L==1 -- list of locators (varint count then
 +---------------+                 <utf8;z8> entries; each locator string
                                   is a single-byte-length UTF-8)
 ~  [HelloExts]  ~    if Z==1 -- extension chain
 +---------------+

 (*) WhatAmI (low 2 bits): 0b00 Router, 0b01 Peer, 0b10 Client, 0b11 reserved.

If L==0, the receiver treats the UDP source address of the HELLO packet as the implicit single locator; this class returns an empty locator list in that case and leaves it to the caller (typically PureJavaZenohScout) to synthesise the source-address locator.

Scouting id space is disjoint from the transport layer; HELLO's id 0x02 happens to match the transport-layer OPEN's id but the two are demultiplexed by their transport and never coexist in the same byte stream.

    Description
    static final int
    FLAG_L
    L flag on the header byte: 1 = explicit locator list follows.

    Z flag on the header byte: 1 = extension chain follows.

    ID
    Scouting-message id byte for HELLO.
    static final int
    MAX_LOCATOR_LEN
    Maximum length in bytes of a single locator string on the wire.
